findings.description: AC-15 (voicemail access) and AC-16 (call type tagging) lack explicit Given/When/Then format
findings.recommendation: Reformat into structured GWT acceptance criteria

findings.description: Phase 1 dependency on NGPSTE-132 deployment not time-bound. No target date for NGPSTE-132 orgsyncng deployment.
findings.recommendation: Add specific target date for NGPSTE-132 deployment as Phase 1 gate

caveats: Some ACs not in Given/When/Then format

findings.description: ADR-005 assumes Lead.Phone field is indexed for selective SOQL queries. Index status not verified in DevInt2. Non-selective query on Lead with >100K records would hit governor limits.
findings.recommendation: Query DevInt2 to verify Phone field index on Lead object

findings.description: ADR-006 VoiceCallActivityHandler does not specify WhoId resolution strategy for PersonAccount. PersonAccount.PersonContactId must be used as WhoId (Task does not accept Account as WhoId).
findings.recommendation: Add PersonAccount → PersonContactId mapping to ADR-006

findings.description: No sequence diagram for the complete inbound call flow (Connect IVR → Queue → Agent → Screen Pop → Task Creation)
findings.recommendation: Add Mermaid sequence diagram for the primary Inside Sales call flow

reRetrievalQueries: Verify Lead.Phone field index status in DevInt2

findings.description: No bulk test for InsideSalesScreenPopResolver handling 200+ concurrent screen pop requests
findings.recommendation: Add T-33 for bulk screen pop resolution under load

findings.description: Apex classes are pseudo-code only. InsideSalesScreenPopResolver, VoiceCallActivityHandler, and RecordingOptOutController are not deployable without full implementation.
findings.recommendation: Flag as Phase 1/2 implementation tasks. Include Apex code in next iteration when NGPSTE-132 is deployed.

findings.description: Queue definition XML does not include queue membership (which users/profiles are assigned). Requires manual configuration.
findings.recommendation: Document queue membership assignment in deployment runbook

refinementDirectives: Implement full Apex classes when NGPSTE-132 base is available
Add queue membership to deployment runbook
caveats: Queue membership not defined in metadata

findings.description: All 23 tests marked NOT RUN. No deployment has occurred. NGPSTE-132 is a prerequisite that has not been deployed to orgsyncng.
findings.recommendation: Execute deployment of NGPSTE-132 metadata first, then NGASIM-76 metadata, then run tests

caveats: All tests NOT RUN — NGPSTE-132 and NGASIM-76 deployment pending

findings.description: Multi-object screen pop exposes Lead and Patient data to Inside Sales agents. Lead may contain PII (email, phone). Patient records may contain PHI (medical device info). FLS must be strictly enforced per IS_CTI_Base permission set.
findings.recommendation: Audit FLS on Lead and PersonAccount for IS_CTI_Base. Restrict sensitive fields (DOB, Medical_Device_Serial__c) from screen pop response.

findings.description: Recording opt-out mechanism (ADR-008) uses Named Credential that does not yet exist. Without Named Credential, RecordingOptOutController would fail. HIPAA compliance requires this mechanism to be functional before go-live.
findings.recommendation: Create Named Credential for Amazon Connect API as a Phase 1 prerequisite. Block go-live without functional opt-out.

findings.description: VoiceCallActivityHandler auto-creates Task records with caller information. Task.Subject may contain caller name (PII). Ensure Task sharing model restricts access to owning agent and manager.
findings.recommendation: Review OWD for Task object. Ensure private or controlled sharing.

refinementDirectives: Audit FLS on IS_CTI_Base permission set for Lead and PersonAccount
Create Named Credential for Amazon Connect API
Review Task OWD and sharing rules
reRetrievalQueries: Check Named Credential for Amazon Connect in DevInt2
caveats: Patient PHI exposure through screen pop unmitigated
